# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder_14b)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Python runtime as a parent image
FROM python:3.8-slim

# Set the working directory in the container
WORKDIR /app

# Copy the current directory contents into the container at /app
COPY . /app

# Install any needed packages specified in requirements.txt
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Make port 80 available to the world outside this container
EXPOSE 80

# Run app.py when the container launches
CMD ["python", "web_server.py"]

# Use an official Node.js runtime as a parent image for API server
FROM node:14-alpine AS api-server

# Set the working directory in the container
WORKDIR /app

# Copy the current directory contents into the container at /app
COPY . /app

# Install any needed packages specified in package.json
RUN npm install

# Make port 8080 available to the world outside this container
EXPOSE 8080

# Run server.js when the container launches
CMD ["node", "api_server.js"]

# Use an official PostgreSQL image for database
FROM postgres:13

# Set environment variables for PostgreSQL
ENV POSTGRES_DB=mydatabase
ENV POSTGRES_USER=myuser
ENV POSTGRES_PASSWORD=mypassword

# Expose port 5432
EXPOSE 5432

# Start the PostgreSQL server
CMD ["postgres"]

# Use an official Redis image for Redis
FROM redis:6.0-alpine

# Expose port 6379
EXPOSE 6379

# Start Redis server
CMD ["redis-server"]
